task_completion.description: Create a 'Schedule' sheet allocating orders to production lines
task_completion.visual_evidence: A sheet named 'Schedule' is visible in the sheet tabs. The sheet contains columns for orders, assigned production line, start date, end date, and slack days.

task_completion.description: Calculate production dates and slack days for all orders
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Schedule' sheet is populated with 15 rows of orders, showing calculated start/end dates and numerical values in the slack days column.

task_completion.description: Create a 'Utilization' sheet with weekly percentages and conditional formatting
task_completion.visual_evidence: A sheet named 'Utilization' exists showing 4 weeks of data for the 4 production lines. The percentage cells are color-coded: red for values > 90%, yellow for 70-90%, and green for < 70%.

task_completion.description: Add a stacked bar chart visualizing weekly utilization
task_completion.visual_evidence: A stacked bar chart is visible on the 'Utilization' sheet, plotting the weekly utilization percentages across the production lines.

task_completion.description: Create a 'Revenue_Analysis' sheet calculating revenue, costs, and profit margins
task_completion.visual_evidence: A sheet named 'Revenue_Analysis' exists. It contains calculated values for expected revenue per order, total revenue by production line, total production cost per line, and profit margin per line.

task_completion.description: Save the completed workbook as production_planning.xlsx
task_completion.visual_evidence: The title bar of the spreadsheet application displays 'production_planning.xlsx', or a file manager/terminal shows the file successfully saved in the /home/ga/Documents/ directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the spreadsheet application GUI to complete the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a spreadsheet application (like LibreOffice Calc) open and being actively interacted with, rather than just a terminal running a Python script to generate the file.

integrity.description: The stacked bar chart is a native spreadsheet chart
integrity.visual_evidence: The chart appears as a native embedded object with standard spreadsheet chart formatting and elements, not an imported static image.

integrity.description: Conditional formatting was applied using built-in features
integrity.visual_evidence: The red/yellow/green colors in the Utilization sheet match standard conditional formatting palettes and are applied consistently based on the cell values, rather than appearing manually painted.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The workbook contains 4 production lines and 15 distinct orders totaling 21,080 units. The calendar spans 4 weeks in January 2025 with exactly 19 working days. The agent must create exactly three new sheets: 'Schedule', 'Utilization', and 'Revenue_Analysis'. The 'Utilization' sheet must include conditional formatting (red > 90%, yellow 70-90%, green < 70%) and a stacked bar chart. The 'Revenue_Analysis' sheet must calculate revenue per order, revenue per line, production cost per line, and profit margin per line.
